summary: Tested Admin Auto-Updater backend (5 endpoints) and frontend (AdminUpdatesPanel) for StreamVault. All 13 backend pytest cases pass. Frontend Playwright e2e verifies up-to-date block, changelog, history (3 rows), job status with log_tail, rollback button click → POST /api/admin/updates/rollback with previous_sha → 200 + sonner toast.

minor.issue: Successful rollback returns the same message string ('Update queued — host watcher fires within ~2s.') as /apply. Mode-aware messaging would be friendlier (e.g. 'Rollback queued — ...'). Frontend toast therefore reads 'Update queued' instead of 'Rollback queued' on rollback. update_manager._enqueue_request() should branch on payload['mode'].

action_items: (Optional, minor) update_manager._enqueue_request: return 'Rollback queued — ...' when payload['mode'] == 'rollback' so the toast reads 'Rollback queued' as suggested in the spec. Current 'or similar' fallback is functional but the request explicitly mentioned the desired wording.

Note for next testing agent: my pytest run (TestUpdatesApply.test_apply_admin_queues) overwrites /tmp/fake_host_repo/.update-state/status.json to 'queued', which hides rollback buttons in the UI (isBusy=true). I reset status.json back to the seeded 'success' state before frontend tests. Re-running the backend suite will require a similar reset before frontend rollback-button verification.
critical_code_review_comments: AdminUpdatesPanel correctly hides rollback button when isBusy (status=queued/running) — good UX safeguard.
rollbackTo() correctly auto-accepts via window.confirm and surfaces res.data.message via toast.success.
Backend update_manager.request_rollback validates target_sha length>=7 and returns ok:false (HTTP 200) for invalid input — consistent with frontend handling that reads res.data.ok.
All 5 admin endpoints enforce role==admin and return 403 for non-admin (verified for /check, /status, /history, /apply, /rollback).
Cosmetic: rollback success message string is shared with apply path in update_manager._enqueue_request — could be mode-aware.
updated_files: /tmp/fake_host_repo/.update-state/status.json (reset to seeded 'success' after apply test mutated it; this is the test sandbox file, not app code)
test_credentials: admin@streamvault.com / Admin123!  (also used progamer@demo.com / Demo123! for non-admin 403 tests)
context_for_next_testing_agent: AdminUpdatesPanel mounted at /admin (AdminPage.jsx line 573). Backend uses HOST_REPO_PATH=/tmp/fake_host_repo. The fake repo's git rev-parse origin/main fails → behind=0, so the up-to-date block always renders. status.json is mutated by /apply and /rollback to 'queued'; reset it back to {"status":"success",...} before re-running frontend rollback-button tests, otherwise canRollback evaluates false and rollback buttons disappear.
rca of the issue: N/A — no defects blocking the user story. Single minor cosmetic note on toast wording captured above.
